claude_workflows.cliff_notes: Agent teams are trending as the standard for multi-agent orchestration. The pattern is one orchestrator plus many specialized teammates coordinating via shared task lists and explicit message passing. Unlike sub-agents which see only seeded context, agent teams share state and work on common tasks in parallel, which maps directly to your swarm architecture needs. Separately, plan-driven development with confidence gates is becoming the anti-pattern to over-engineering â€” create phases, review before committing, and instruct Claude to ask clarifying questions when confidence drops below 90%. On infrastructure, Ghosty terminal is replacing IDE-based Claude Code for scalingâ€”developers report running hundreds of concurrent agent sessions without performance degradation.
key_insights: Agent teams with shared task lists and inter-agent messaging now outperform sub-agents for orchestration where agents need visibility into each other's work
Confidence-gated clarification (ask when <90% confident) prevents implementation divergence in autonomous decision systems
Plan-first with commit gates between phases prevents accumulation of unfixed problems across swarm algorithm iterations
Multi-option exploration before commitment aligns with evolutionary optimization and competitive strategy discovery workflows

gemini_platform.cliff_notes: Gemini 2.0 Flash experimental model is now rolling out to all Gemini users, bringing faster inference and broader availability for game generation workloads. Alongside this, Google has introduced Deep Research, a new capability that combines advanced reasoning with extended context handling to function as an intelligent research assistant, potentially useful for procedural game design and mechanics exploration. Separately, Gemini 3 has introduced a Thinking mode that performs deeper logical reasoning akin to chain-of-thought, delivering higher quality responses for complex tasks. These updates improve both speed and reasoning depth across the Gemini lineup, making them more suitable for iterative game creation workflows.
key_insights: Gemini 2.0 Flash now generally available to all users. Prioritize this model for game generation tasks requiring lower latency and broad user access.
Deep Research feature enables AI-assisted exploration of game mechanics and design patterns with extended context. Consider using this for conceptual design phases of game generation pipeline.
Gemini 3 Thinking mode provides deeper logical reasoning for complex game logic synthesis. Enable when generating intricate game rules, level algorithms, or decision trees.
Monitor regional access variations (noted in China-specific threads). Plan fallback routing if targeting international users.
